streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: phenylacetylene as dipolarophile in DES, kept separate from nitroacetate until mixing to control stoichiometry and prevent premature nitrile oxide generation
From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: ethyl nitroacetate as 1,3-dipole precursor in DES, 2.0 equiv to compensate for nitrile oxide dimerization, mixed just before heated reactor

mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Phenylacetylene and ethyl nitroacetate mixed at T-mixer immediately before heated coil. Premixing at room temperature acceptable since nitrile oxide generation requires 120°C. Both streams combined then enter heated zone simultaneously to ensure nitrile oxide generated in presence of dipolarophile to minimize dimerization.
chemistry_notes: Thermal 1,3-dipolar cycloaddition driven by in-situ nitrile oxide generation from ethyl nitroacetate at 120°C. Key design decisions: (1) Two-stream setup prevents premature nitrile oxide formation and dimerization. (2) Rapid heating in narrow-bore tubing provides excellent heat transfer for DES (viscous at RT, ~5-10 cP at 120°C). (3) Short residence time (0.8 min vs 15 min batch) enabled by superior mixing and heat transfer. (4) BPR required due to potential water elimination during nitrile oxide formation. (5) Calculations show mass-transfer limitation (Da=39) - T-mixer critical for rapid mixing before reaction zone.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Calculation estimated 0.8 min (range 0.5-2.2 min). Analogy 1 used 5 min for photocycloaddition at 200°C - different mechanism but similar thermal activation. Analogy 3 used 60 min at 60°C for nucleophilic ring-opening - much slower reaction. Using calculated 0.8 min as it matches the high-temperature thermal activation pattern.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Calculation: 0.5 mL/min total (0.25 + 0.25) to achieve 0.8 min residence time in 0.38 mL reactor volume. Verified: τ = V/Q = 0.38/0.5 = 0.76 min ≈ 0.8 min.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Same as batch (120°C) - Chemistry Plan indicates this temperature required for nitrile oxide generation. Analogy 1 used 200°C but for different photochemical mechanism. Analogy 2 used 154°C for energetic compound synthesis - higher than needed here.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Calculation result: 0.0667 M in reactor after mixing. Lower than batch (0.5 M) due to dilution from two-stream setup, but adequate for reaction based on Chemistry Plan mechanism analysis.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation: BPR required at 5.0 bar due to gas generation (water elimination during nitrile oxide formation). Analogy 1 used 34 bar, Analogy 2 used 47.6 bar - both much higher than needed here. Using calculated 5.0 bar.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA selected for high temperature compatibility (120°C) and chemical resistance to DES. Not photochemical so transparency not required. Analogy 1 used steel coil but that's for 200°C operation.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: 1.0 mm from calculation to achieve target residence time and manage DES viscosity. Analogy 3 used 1.2 mm for similar viscous system - close agreement. Calculation shows Pe=9 (axial dispersion concern) but acceptable for this reaction.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: 0.38 mL from calculation: V = τ × Q = 0.8 min × 0.5 mL/min. Analogy 1 used 30 mL but for much longer residence time (5 min) and higher flow rate (6 mL/min).
